message: packet bounds check is narrower than the later packet access: invalid access to packet, off=16 size=2, R1(id=1,off=16,r=17); R1 offset is outside of the packet
required_proof: prove R1 has packet range at least 18 bytes before this access; verifier currently has range 17

related_spans.label: verifier only proves packet range 0 bytes on this path, but the rejected access requires 18 bytes

evidence.detail: invalid access to packet, off=16 size=2, R1(id=1,off=16,r=17); R1 offset is outside of the packet
source has a packet bounds check, but verifier state after that check proves only a shorter packet range than the rejected access needs

help: Move the data_end check to the final pointer expression and include the access width, for example check pointer + size before dereferencing pointer.
Add or move a packet bounds check immediately before the access or helper argument use.
Check the exact pointer and byte length passed to the helper, not only an earlier header pointer.
